concepts.label: Christianity
concepts.definition: Abrahamic monotheistic religion based on the life and teachings of Jesus Christ

concepts.label: Islam
concepts.definition: Abrahamic monotheistic religion founded by Prophet Muhammad in 7th century Arabia

concepts.label: Buddhism
concepts.definition: Indian religion and philosophy based on teachings of Siddhartha Gautama (the Buddha)

concepts.label: Hinduism
concepts.definition: Major religion originating in the Indian subcontinent, characterized by belief in Brahman and dharma

concepts.label: Judaism
concepts.definition: Abrahamic monotheistic religion of the Jewish people, based on the Torah

concepts.label: Sikhism
concepts.definition: Monotheistic religion founded by Guru Nanak in 15th century Punjab

concepts.label: Zoroastrianism
concepts.definition: Ancient Iranian religion founded by Zoroaster, centered on Ahura Mazda

concepts.label: Jainism
concepts.definition: Indian religion emphasizing non-violence (ahimsa), truth, and asceticism

concepts.label: Shinto
concepts.definition: Indigenous religion of Japan involving worship of kami (spirits)

concepts.label: Taoism
concepts.definition: Chinese philosophical and religious tradition emphasizing living in harmony with the Tao

concepts.label: Confucianism
concepts.definition: Chinese ethical and philosophical system based on teachings of Confucius

concepts.label: Baha'i Faith
concepts.definition: Religion founded by Baha'u'llah in 19th century Persia teaching unity of all religions

concepts.label: Animism

concepts.label: Paganism
concepts.definition: Umbrella term for polytheistic and nature-based religious traditions

concepts.label: Wicca
concepts.definition: Modern pagan witchcraft religion founded by Gerald Gardner in mid-20th century

concepts.label: Rastafari

concepts.label: Druze

concepts.label: Tenrikyo
concepts.definition: Japanese new religion founded by Nakayama Miki in 1838

concepts.label: Cao Dai

concepts.label: Mandaeism

concepts.label: Jesus Christ
concepts.definition: Central figure of Christianity, believed by Christians to be the Son of God and Messiah

concepts.label: God the Father
concepts.definition: First person of the Christian Trinity, the creator God

concepts.label: Holy Spirit
concepts.definition: Third person of the Christian Trinity, God's active force in the world

concepts.label: Holy Trinity
concepts.definition: Christian doctrine that God exists as three persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit

concepts.label: Virgin Mary
concepts.definition: Mother of Jesus Christ, venerated in Christianity especially in Catholicism

concepts.label: Saint Joseph
concepts.definition: Husband of Mary and earthly father of Jesus, patron saint of workers

concepts.label: Apostle Peter

concepts.label: Apostle Paul
concepts.definition: Early Christian apostle who wrote much of the New Testament, formerly Saul of Tarsus

concepts.label: Apostle John
concepts.definition: One of the twelve apostles, traditionally credited with the Gospel of John and Revelation

concepts.label: Apostle James
concepts.definition: Son of Zebedee, one of the twelve apostles, first apostle to be martyred

concepts.label: Apostle Andrew
concepts.definition: Brother of Peter, one of the first disciples called by Jesus

concepts.label: Apostle Matthew
concepts.definition: Tax collector who became one of the twelve apostles, traditionally authored the Gospel of Matthew

concepts.label: Apostle Thomas
concepts.definition: One of the twelve apostles, known as Doubting Thomas for questioning the resurrection

concepts.label: Apostle Philip
concepts.definition: One of the twelve apostles from Bethsaida

concepts.label: Apostle Bartholomew
concepts.definition: One of the twelve apostles, sometimes identified with Nathanael

concepts.label: Apostle Thaddaeus
concepts.definition: One of the twelve apostles, also called Judas son of James

concepts.label: Apostle Simon the Zealot
concepts.definition: One of the twelve apostles, distinguished from Simon Peter

concepts.label: Judas Iscariot
concepts.definition: Apostle who betrayed Jesus for thirty pieces of silver

concepts.label: Pontius Pilate

concepts.label: John the Baptist
concepts.definition: Jewish prophet who baptized Jesus and preached repentance, forerunner of Christ

concepts.label: Crucifixion of Jesus

concepts.label: Resurrection of Jesus
concepts.definition: Christian belief that Jesus rose from the dead on the third day after crucifixion

concepts.label: Ascension of Jesus
concepts.definition: Christian belief that Jesus ascended to heaven forty days after resurrection

concepts.label: Second Coming
concepts.definition: Christian belief in the future return of Jesus Christ to earth

concepts.label: Original Sin
concepts.definition: Christian doctrine that humanity inherited a sinful nature from Adam and Eve's fall

concepts.label: Salvation
concepts.definition: Christian concept of deliverance from sin and its consequences through faith in Christ

concepts.label: Baptism
concepts.definition: Christian sacrament of initiation using water, symbolizing purification and rebirth

concepts.label: Eucharist
concepts.definition: Christian sacrament commemorating the Last Supper with bread and wine

concepts.label: Last Supper
concepts.definition: Final meal Jesus shared with his apostles before his crucifixion

concepts.label: Transubstantiation
concepts.definition: Catholic doctrine that bread and wine become the body and blood of Christ during Eucharist

concepts.label: Catholic Church
concepts.definition: Largest Christian church led by the Pope, with over 1 billion members worldwide

concepts.label: Eastern Orthodox Church
concepts.definition: Second largest Christian church, split from Rome in the Great Schism of 1054

concepts.label: Protestantism
concepts.definition: Branch of Christianity originating from the 16th century Reformation

concepts.label: Martin Luther
concepts.definition: German theologian who initiated the Protestant Reformation with his 95 Theses in 1517

concepts.label: John Calvin
concepts.definition: French theologian and reformer, key figure in Calvinism and Reformed tradition

concepts.label: Anglican Communion
concepts.definition: Christian denomination originating from the Church of England under Henry VIII

concepts.label: Baptist Church
concepts.definition: Protestant denomination emphasizing believer's baptism by full immersion

concepts.label: Methodist Church
concepts.definition: Protestant denomination founded by John Wesley in 18th century England

concepts.label: Lutheran Church
concepts.definition: Protestant denomination following the theology of Martin Luther

concepts.label: Pentecostalism
concepts.definition: Christian movement emphasizing baptism in the Holy Spirit and speaking in tongues

concepts.label: Mormonism
concepts.definition: Restorationist Christian denomination founded by Joseph Smith in 1830

concepts.label: Jehovah's Witnesses
concepts.definition: Christian denomination known for door-to-door evangelism and rejection of blood transfusions

concepts.label: Seventh-day Adventist
concepts.definition: Protestant denomination observing Saturday Sabbath and expecting Second Coming

concepts.label: Coptic Christianity
concepts.definition: Ancient Christian tradition of Egypt, one of the oldest Christian communities

concepts.label: Great Schism
concepts.definition: Split between Eastern Orthodox and Roman Catholic churches in 1054 CE

concepts.label: Protestant Reformation
concepts.definition: 16th century religious movement challenging Catholic Church authority, led by Luther

concepts.label: Nicene Creed

concepts.label: Council of Nicaea
concepts.definition: First ecumenical council in 325 CE establishing core Christian doctrines

concepts.label: Immaculate Conception

concepts.label: Assumption of Mary
concepts.definition: Catholic dogma that Mary was taken bodily into heaven at end of earthly life

concepts.label: Purgatory
concepts.definition: Catholic doctrine of an intermediate state of purification after death

concepts.label: Papal Infallibility
concepts.definition: Catholic dogma that the Pope cannot err when speaking ex cathedra on faith and morals

concepts.label: Ten Commandments

concepts.label: Sermon on the Mount
concepts.definition: Collection of Jesus' teachings including the Beatitudes, from Gospel of Matthew

concepts.label: Beatitudes
concepts.definition: Eight blessings taught by Jesus in the Sermon on the Mount

concepts.label: Lord's Prayer
concepts.definition: Christian prayer taught by Jesus to his disciples, beginning 'Our Father'

concepts.label: Parable of the Good Samaritan

concepts.label: Parable of the Prodigal Son
concepts.definition: Jesus' parable about God's forgiveness and a father welcoming back his wayward son

concepts.label: Garden of Eden
concepts.definition: Biblical paradise where God placed Adam and Eve before the Fall

concepts.label: Adam and Eve
concepts.definition: First man and woman created by God according to Abrahamic tradition

concepts.label: Noah's Ark
concepts.definition: Biblical vessel built by Noah to save his family and animals from the Great Flood

concepts.label: Tower of Babel
concepts.definition: Biblical story explaining the origin of different languages

concepts.label: Moses
concepts.definition: Hebrew prophet who led the Israelites out of Egypt and received the Ten Commandments

concepts.label: Abraham
concepts.definition: Patriarch of Judaism, Christianity, and Islam, father of Isaac and Ishmael

concepts.label: King David
concepts.definition: Second king of Israel, slayer of Goliath, author of many Psalms

concepts.label: King Solomon
concepts.definition: Son of David, king of Israel known for wisdom and building the First Temple

concepts.label: Prophet Elijah
concepts.definition: Hebrew prophet who confronted the prophets of Baal and ascended to heaven in a chariot of fire

concepts.label: Prophet Isaiah
concepts.definition: Hebrew prophet whose writings predicted the coming of the Messiah

concepts.label: Prophet Jeremiah
concepts.definition: Hebrew prophet who warned of the destruction of Jerusalem

concepts.label: Prophet Ezekiel
concepts.definition: Hebrew prophet who had visions of God's throne and the valley of dry bones

concepts.label: Prophet Daniel
concepts.definition: Hebrew prophet who survived the lion's den and interpreted dreams

concepts.label: Archangel Michael

concepts.label: Archangel Gabriel
concepts.definition: Angel who announced the birth of Jesus and revealed the Quran to Muhammad

concepts.label: Archangel Raphael
concepts.definition: Angel of healing mentioned in the Book of Tobit

concepts.label: Satan
concepts.definition: Adversary figure in Abrahamic religions, the devil and tempter of humanity

concepts.label: Lucifer
concepts.definition: Name associated with Satan, meaning 'light-bearer', a fallen angel

concepts.label: Heaven
concepts.definition: Abrahamic concept of the abode of God and the blessed afterlife

concepts.label: Hell
concepts.definition: Abrahamic concept of a place of punishment for the wicked after death

concepts.label: Apocalypse
concepts.definition: End-times prophecy described in the Book of Revelation

concepts.label: Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse
concepts.definition: Symbolic figures in Revelation representing conquest, war, famine, and death

concepts.label: Muhammad
concepts.definition: Prophet and founder of Islam, considered the last prophet in Islamic tradition (570-632 CE)

concepts.label: Allah
concepts.definition: Arabic word for God, the supreme deity in Islam

concepts.label: Five Pillars of Islam
concepts.definition: Five core practices: Shahada, Salat, Zakat, Sawm, and Hajj

concepts.label: Salat
concepts.definition: Islamic ritual prayer performed five times daily facing Mecca

concepts.label: Zakat

concepts.label: Sawm
concepts.definition: Islamic fasting during the month of Ramadan from dawn to sunset

concepts.label: Hajj
concepts.definition: Islamic pilgrimage to Mecca required at least once in a lifetime

concepts.label: Kaaba

concepts.label: Mecca

concepts.label: Medina
concepts.definition: Second holiest city in Islam, location of Muhammad's mosque and tomb

concepts.label: Jerusalem in Islam

concepts.label: Al-Aqsa Mosque

concepts.label: Dome of the Rock
concepts.definition: Islamic shrine on Temple Mount in Jerusalem, site of Muhammad's Night Journey

concepts.label: Sunni Islam
concepts.definition: Largest branch of Islam comprising about 85-90% of Muslims worldwide

concepts.label: Shia Islam
concepts.definition: Second largest branch of Islam, believing Ali was Muhammad's rightful successor

concepts.label: Sufism

concepts.definition: Distinct branch of Islam, neither Sunni nor Shia, predominant in Oman

concepts.label: Uthman ibn Affan

concepts.label: Khadijah

concepts.label: Fatimah

concepts.label: Sharia

concepts.label: Hadith

concepts.label: Sunnah

concepts.label: Fatwa

concepts.label: Jihad
concepts.definition: Islamic concept meaning 'struggle', both internal spiritual and external defense

concepts.label: Isra and Mi'raj

concepts.label: Jannah
concepts.definition: Islamic concept of paradise, the garden of eternal bliss

concepts.label: Jahannam
concepts.definition: Islamic concept of hell, place of punishment for sinners

concepts.definition: Islamic belief in a final day when all will be judged by Allah

concepts.label: Mosque
concepts.definition: Islamic place of worship and community gathering

concepts.label: Wudu

concepts.label: Haram

concepts.label: Hijab

concepts.label: Umrah

concepts.label: Al-Ghazali

concepts.label: Ibn Sina (Avicenna)

concepts.label: Tawhid
concepts.definition: Islamic concept of the oneness and unity of God

concepts.label: Tirthankara
concepts.definition: Spiritual teacher in Jainism who has conquered samsara, 24 in the current cycle

concepts.label: Five Ks of Sikhism
concepts.definition: Five articles of faith worn by Khalsa Sikhs: Kesh, Kangha, Kara, Kachera, Kirpan

concepts.label: Khalsa
concepts.definition: Community of initiated Sikhs established by Guru Gobind Singh in 1699

concepts.label: Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib)
concepts.definition: Holiest gurdwara of Sikhism, located in Amritsar, India

concepts.label: Langar
concepts.definition: Community kitchen in Sikhism serving free meals to all regardless of background

concepts.label: Tao
concepts.definition: The way or path, fundamental concept in Chinese philosophy and Taoism

concepts.label: Wu Wei
concepts.definition: Taoist concept of effortless action, going with the natural flow

concepts.label: Qi
concepts.definition: Vital life force or energy in Chinese philosophy and traditional medicine

concepts.label: Feng Shui
concepts.definition: Chinese geomantic practice of arranging space for harmonious energy flow

concepts.label: Filial Piety
concepts.definition: Confucian virtue of respect and care for parents and ancestors

concepts.label: Ren (Benevolence)
concepts.definition: Central Confucian virtue meaning humaneness or compassion toward others

concepts.label: Li (Ritual)
concepts.definition: Confucian concept of proper conduct, ritual propriety, and social norms

concepts.label: Nirvana Day
concepts.definition: Buddhist holiday marking the death and final nirvana of the historical Buddha

concepts.label: Kathina
concepts.definition: Buddhist festival marking the end of the rainy season retreat for monks

concepts.label: Makha Bucha
concepts.definition: Thai Buddhist holiday celebrating a spontaneous gathering of 1,250 monks before Buddha

concepts.label: Songkran
concepts.definition: Thai New Year water festival with Buddhist origins

concepts.label: Obon
concepts.definition: Japanese Buddhist festival honoring the spirits of ancestors

concepts.label: Ulambana
concepts.definition: Buddhist festival of offering to relieve suffering of departed spirits

concepts.label: Saga Dawa
concepts.definition: Tibetan Buddhist holy month commemorating the Buddha's birth, enlightenment, and death

concepts.label: Hagia Sophia
concepts.definition: Former Christian cathedral and mosque in Istanbul, now a museum, architectural masterpiece

concepts.label: Church of the Holy Sepulchre
concepts.definition: Church in Jerusalem built over the site of Jesus' crucifixion and burial

concepts.label: Sistine Chapel
concepts.definition: Chapel in the Vatican known for Michelangelo's ceiling and Last Judgment fresco

concepts.label: Wailing Wall
concepts.definition: Alternative name for the Western Wall in Jerusalem

concepts.label: Temple Mount
concepts.definition: Holy site in Jerusalem sacred to Judaism, Christianity, and Islam

concepts.label: Bodh Gaya
concepts.definition: Site in India where the Buddha attained enlightenment, holiest Buddhist pilgrimage site

concepts.label: Lumbini
concepts.definition: Birthplace of the Buddha in modern Nepal, UNESCO World Heritage Site

concepts.label: Mount Sinai
concepts.definition: Mountain where Moses received the Ten Commandments according to the Bible

concepts.label: River Jordan
concepts.definition: River in the Middle East where Jesus was baptized by John the Baptist

concepts.label: Bethlehem
concepts.definition: City where Jesus was born according to Christian tradition

concepts.label: Nazareth
concepts.definition: City in Galilee where Jesus grew up

concepts.label: Santiago de Compostela
concepts.definition: Spanish city and major Christian pilgrimage destination housing relics of Saint James

concepts.label: Lourdes
concepts.definition: French town and Catholic pilgrimage site where Saint Bernadette saw visions of Mary

concepts.label: Fatima
concepts.definition: Portuguese town where three children reported visions of the Virgin Mary in 1917
